slug: 北极熊不是唯一受害者_气候变化对穷人最残酷
title: 北极熊不是唯一受害者——气候变化对穷人最残酷

outline.concept: Physical exposure asymmetry: poor people are concentrated in climate-vulnerable geographies—flood plains, urban heat islands, drought-prone farmland—and work weather-exposed jobs like farming, construction, street vending. Climate change doesn't create their risk from scratch; it weaponizes the vulnerability already built into where they live and what they do.
outline.query: 暴雨 洪水 老家 房子 农活 户外 高温 outdoor work

outline.concept: Adaptation is a paywall: every climate defense—air conditioning, reinforced housing, flood insurance, relocation—costs money. The wealthy buy buffers between themselves and extreme weather; the poor absorb the impact directly because they can't afford the buffer layer.
outline.query: 空调 装不起 房租 修房 保险 买不起 afford 搬家

outline.concept: The compounding shock spiral: climate disasters destroy productive assets poor people can't replace—crops, tools, livestock, housing. Recovery means debt; debt erodes future resilience; each hit makes the next one worse. This is the climate poverty trap: not a single disaster but a cascade that accelerates downward.
outline.query: 灾后 损失 借钱 还债 绝收 重建 crops destroyed debt

outline.concept: Emission-consequence inversion: the populations who contribute least to global carbon emissions—subsistence farmers, slum residents, small island communities—suffer the most severe climate consequences. The highest per-capita emitters can afford to insulate themselves from the damage they disproportionately cause.
outline.query: 工厂 排污 村庄 污染 出国 发达国家 差距 factory village
